messages.message: Damn, china might actually end up doing the whole socialism thing
I still dont think this is any indication of the feasibility of this in the west though since they did it with only ethnic chinese ciitzens
I think benevolent socialism is actually probably increasingly easy to do in 2026 if you have a >90% ethnically homogenous population with an iq over 95.

Also i think the average iq part is probably way morr significant than the racial homogeneity part (Googleplex  is a good example, racially preferential hiring practices among both Indian and Chinese not withstanding)
I think once a group average iq surpasses 130 in a ethnic homogeneity isnt necessary anymore for high trust coordination

Yeah, seems like he has a few objective points that are not well integrated between each other or what others experience and functionally, they are being used to justify stasis.

Hunger is interesting in how there are so many ways to manage it. Ghrelin is particularly interesting here that losing weight tends to be uncomfortable until a new stable weight is reached which changes the ghrelin response.
I also can't help but think about the happiness of fish parable but the hunger of Yud going on here 😆

https://x.com/i/status/2077008858367562188

This feels like a brewing internet argument

> the Olympic Games were one of the most powerful unifying forces in the ancient Greek world, creating a shared sense of Hellenic identity among fiercely independent and often warring city-states

we need to play

or die trying
how do we play well? and don't die?
Amazing how much of this argument seems to boil down to markets can decide or not decide.
And by markets, this could be extended to people in general.
this is dramatic, no?
Well, you keep others in the game, keeping them from dying too hard. That's the primary responsibility of an infinite game player.

There's some acceptance to them also setting rules but if you can't accept the rules, playing with them becomes restricted. This is something I'm working with my kids on. Common for 6yo to be assigned some imaginary role she doesn't want by 4yo and their negotiating can get stuck.

Yesterday 6yo wanted to be a "pink lunar rabbit" while 4yo demanded she was a yellow one. Completely absurd from my level, but I pointed out if 4yo has rules 6yo doesn't want to follow the game will split and she'll have to play alone.
So, often they play alone until it gets boring and they make up a whole new game or find some compromise that keeps the game between each other going.
Went great!
What you do not who you are. 😉 Verbs tend to be more accurate than nouns.

For me <@416623820917440512> I just don't get what you are getting at. As in, I don't know what the context that this model/frame/definition set is useful for. 

Do you have a clear application you're thinking of?

messages.message: "are you there? we have a universe between us"
Eerie. Once in meditation a loud overwhelming auditory hallucination came to me that said: "it's so hard to reach you with a universe between us"
<@1250084478152478773> did you write about the explainer and the breaker concepts? i thought you did but now i cant find the source material
i've now been fasting for like 6 days so im closer to the source so the message reached me too 😎
Hmmm so much in that the cat was made that big because of the attention bandwidth and particular quality it was given. Wanting it to be on the bed and trying to give it space balloons the size.

yeah the thing thats isomomorphic is the set of possible behaviors

whether the cat is massive or small the result is the same, you can't use that part of the bed
The thing about values space and it's relativity is that anything can be resized to whatever you can feel/ perceive/imagine
Similar when someone minimizes another's experience or inflates it.

i wanted to create a graphic of e.g. a room and then multiple impressionistic paintings of it that represents different worlds or viewpoints

and then link that to e.g. starwars and say "fiction is an impressionistic account of how the world is or could be"

to hammer in the part that there are many worlds within this world
someone else was recording when i was making the sign, but i wasnt able to figure out who or get the footage 😫
cj the x is a content creator (search on youtube)

this is impressionism too 

not just about size

Awhile ago I saw someone use AI to generate a dining room chair in over 100 different art styles as a demo of how various linguistic styles changed this objective thing and it was pretty fascinating and made me value these exaggerated lenses more.
Yeah, it's impressionism all the way down. If not, you're violating ineffability.
I've never seen an apple that looked exactly like another even in its same variety.
As a kid I spent ridiculous amounts of time comparing rocks and minerals that were "the same" to see both the differences and similarities.
There's something about identifying rocks, plants, and animals that carry over into the rest of life.
Feels sorly missing as people shrug and go "dunno, it's just a bug" and all arthropods look the same to them
Maybe they can see spiders or beetles and whatever, but there's such a low resolution of seeing differences it seems.

That's fine - I think just writing down your intentions is sufficient (like whenever we have something blow up and manufacture discourse, it will be very impressive to show a timestamped messaged that says "I am creating this sign and I think it will trigger XYZ") 

It's like casting a spell, preregistation

i've been thinking of the phenomenon of categorization for a while now

i still can't put it into words

but i think its extremely important

e.g. just sorting my music into playlists is an activity i sometimes do while i wait for codex to be done working, and its been months and i've still never found a good way to categorize music

i think of myself as a memetic entomologist, i have this wall of memetic bugs that i've collected and as you say most people just wonder why im repeating myself but im not repeating myself, my resolution of categorization is just high enough that i see differences between two ideas that might be identical to others
i think of categorization in terms of creating a latent space https://x.com/evalladen/status/1943922182045766002?s=20

if you think im joking i tried doing this https://github.com/Mr-Ples/song-categorization

but its too much effort cuz i have thousands of songs
its a program i made that has the features i could initiatially think of to create a low dimensional latent space
is the song positive? negative? etc
To use a SF buzzword, it's a "taste" thing.
Omg the latent space here is massive and includes made up words

yeah my most recent attempt is to reduce the resolution and throw things into more general buckets

which funnily enough is approximating `genres`
turns out people are doing a decent job already
its not perfect tho
Genera from first principles 😆
Ok, but it might be cool to break up genrea and make a family chart. Maybe even showcase extinct members.
sick

the genre of tons of songs are basically up for interpretation and don't really have names

its the nature of the game
Yeah, plus there's a bunch of messy overlap because it could have like 4 or more parents.
Yes Markus buehler

messages.message: oh dang partners with FIRE i'm listening now

It is of course true that taste drives the actions behind thriving, but it is also true that many tastes take one away from thriving, or at least keep one from greater thriving. This is what Christians (esp. Calvinists) would call idolatry. There are as many aesthetics as there are "spirits", and 1 John instructs the believer to *test* the spirits. The nature of the test is to see who confesses that Christ came, died, and rose in the flesh; this is in effect a commitment to that which is truly actionable and winning.
